title: BIOL 105 - Principles of Biology
description: Fundamental processes of life. Structure and function of cells, genetics, evolution, and ecology.

title: BOT 105 - General Botany
description: Structure, function, reproduction, life cycles, and phylogenetic relationships of major plant groups. Relationships of plants to other organisms and to human activities.

title: CHEM 109 - General Chemistry I
description: Fundamental concepts: chemical foundations, stoichiometry, chemical reactions, gases, thermochemistry, atomic theory, bonding, liquids, solutions. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: CHEM 110 - General Chemistry II
description: Fundamental concepts: kinetics; equilibrium; acids and bases; acid-base, solubility, and complex ion equilibria; entropy and free energy; electrochemistry; qualitative analysis. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: MATH 105 - Calculus for the Biological Sciences and Natural Resources
description: Differential and integral calculus. Apply to biological sciences, including exponential growth and decay.

title: MATH 109 - Calculus I
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and their applications.

title: PHYX 106 - College Physics: Mechanics and Heat
description: Non-Calculus, for science majors. Mechanics, fluids, heat, sound.

title: STAT 109 - Introductory Biostatistics
description: Descriptive statistics, probability, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, confidence intervals, contingency tests, regression and correlation, tests of hypothesis, analysis of variance. Emphasis: methods and applications used in the biological and natural resource sciences.

title: ZOOL 110 - Introductory Zoology
description: Structure, function, evolution, and diversity of major groups of animals.

title: BIOL 307 - Evolution
description: Properties and differentiation of populations. Population genetics; mechanisms of species formation; and macroevolution.

title: BIOL 340 - Genetics
description: Principles of heredity; nature and function of genetic material, with quantitative analyses; genetic constitution of populations.

title: CHEM 228 - Brief Organic Chemistry
description: For majors in biological science/natural resource areas. Nomenclature, physical properties, synthesis, and reactions of compounds representing major functional group categories. Reaction mechanisms emphasized.

title: PHYX 107 - College Physics: Electromagnetism and Modern Physics
description: Non-Calculus, for science majors. Geometric optics, electricity, magnetism, electromagnetic waves, AC circuits, physical optics, relativity.

title: PHYX 118 - College Physics: Biological Applications
description: Geometrical optics, simple DC circuits.

title: FISH 320 - Limnology
description: Lake formation and aging. Physical, chemical, and behavioral relationships between organisms and their environments.

title: GEOG 106 - Physical Geography
description: Global patterns of climate, soils, vegetation. Landform geography. Climate regions defined on basis of physical environmental and agricultural land-use parameters. Geography majors must also take GEOG 106L.

title: GEOL 109 - General Geology
description: Physical geology. Origin and constitution of the earth, internal and external processes that determine crustal and surficial features, and methods in investigating and interpreting earth history.

title: OCN 109 - General Oceanography
description: Extent of the oceans; chemical nature of sea water; causes/effects of currents, tides, and waves; animal and plant life in the sea; features of the ocean floor.

title: OCN 109L - General Oceanography Laboratory
description: Introductory laboratory activities to develop understanding of fundamental concepts from geological, chemical, physical, and biological oceanography and the inter-relationship between those concepts.

title: SOIL 260 - Introduction to Soil Science
description: Soil’s physical, chemical, and biological properties. Implications for land management. Identify soil parent materials; use soil survey reports.

title: BIOL 330 - Principles of Ecology
description: Major ideas shaping modern ecology: population regulation, competition, predation, ecosystem energetics, mathematical models, and nutrient cycling. Role of biological and physical factors in developing community structure.

title: BIOL 434 - Population and Community Ecology
description:
The study of the structure and distribution of populations and communities. Topics include population viability modeling, metapopulation dynamics, mark-recapture techniques, species interactions, trophic dynamics, assembly rules, biodiversity, and conservation issues.
Co-listed with: BIOL 534. (Only one of the co-listed courses may be taken for credit. See Terminology for more information.)

title: BIOL 350 - Cell Biology
description: Study of the structure and function of cells with emphases in biochemistry, molecular biology, and physiology, and methods used to address relevant questions in the field.

title: BIOL 440 - Molecular Genetics Lab
description: Experiments in modern and classical genetics, using a variety of organisms.

title: BIOL 450 - Cell Biology Laboratory
description: Experiments in modern and classical cellular and molecular biology, cellular physiology, and biochemistry of cells using cell culture models.

title: BIOL 412 - General Microbiology
description: Natural history and importance of bacteria, archaea, and viruses. Structure, growth, metabolism, genetics, taxonomy, diversity, pathogenesis, and applied aspects of microorganisms.

title: BOT 310 - General Plant Physiology
description: Plant growth, development, reproduction, metabolism, photosynthesis, soil/water relations, inorganic nutrition, and translocation. Quantitative analysis of physiological functions.

title: ZOOL 310 - Animal Physiology
description: Comparative organ system physiology of animals. Adaptive strategies.

title: BOT 350 - Plant Taxonomy
description: Identify ferns, gymnosperms, and flowering plants. Recognize families and key plants in the local flora.

title: BOT 354 - Agrostology
description: Taxonomy, identification, and relationships of grasses of North America.

title: BOT 355 - Lichens and Bryophytes
description: Biology, ecology, natural history, and taxonomy of lichens, liverworts, hornworts, and mosses. Emphasis: epiphytic habitats. Weekend field trips.

title: BOT 356 - Phycology
description:
Biology and evolution of photosynthetic eukaryotes. Marine algal ecology. Field trips, identification skills, micro- and macroalgal sampling, data analysis, scientific writing.
Co-listed with: BOT 556. (Only one of the co-listed courses may be taken for credit. See Terminology for more information.)

title: BOT 358 - Biology of the Microfungi
description: Morphology, genetics, classification, ecology, and economic importance of yeasts and molds. Emphasis on isolation, culture, and lab techniques.

title: BOT 359 - Biology of the Ascomycetes and Basidiomycetes
description: Morphology, anatomy, classification, genetics, ecology, physiology, and economic importance of ascomycetes and basidiomycetes.

title: FISH 310 - Ichthyology
description: Biology of fishes and fishlike vertebrates. Anatomy/concepts of systematics of fishes; classifying fishes, particularly commercial, game, and forage species.

title: WLDF 365 - Ornithology I
description: Classification, life histories, ecology, behavior, and special adaptations of birds. Identification in field and lab.

title: ZOOL 314 - Invertebrate Zoology
description: Comparative functional morphology, life histories, and phylogeny of invertebrates.

title: ZOOL 316 - Freshwater Aquatic Invertebrates
description: Identification, behavior, life history. Insects, crustaceans, mollusks.

title: ZOOL 354 - Herpetology
description: Biology, classification, anatomy, distribution, and life histories of amphibians and reptiles.

title: ZOOL 356 - Mammalogy
description: Comparative mammalian biology. Systematics, morphology, behavior, reproduction, physiology, ecology, zoogeography.

title: ZOOL 358 - General Entomology
description: Classification, identification, anatomy, physiology, ecology, behavior, control of insects.

title: STAT 333 - Linear Regression Models/ANOVA
description: Linear regression, analysis of variance, and other linear models applied to experimental and observational studies. course emphasizes model formulation, assumptions, selection, and interpretation in both hypothesis-testing and descriptive contexts.

title: STAT 406 - Sampling Design and Analysis
description:
Randomized sample surveys are used for natural resource monitoring, election polling, plant abundance estimation, and other purposes. This course presents approaches to sample selection and to inference/estimation from sample data.
Co-listed with: STAT 506. (Only one of the co-listed courses may be taken for credit. See Terminology for more information.)
